commit.message:
EXP-1331-1340: Therapy operationalization — ground truth, DIA validation, simulation

Key findings:
- Physics model has ~25% systematic bias (all methods biased for well-calibrated)
- Overnight glucose drift is the best single signal for basal assessment
- Population DIA = 6.0h median (vs 5.0h profile) with fit R²=0.751
- Dinner CR needs most adjustment (77.3 mg/dL mean excursion, 54% flagged)
- ISF varies 131% within day — single values are insufficient
- 6/11 patients stable over 6 months, 5/11 drifting
- Overnight-only simulation insufficient (TIR Δ=-1.4%, 0/11 improved)
- Need multi-block, multi-parameter approach for meaningful simulation

commit.message:
perf: singleton Val.True/Val.False for runtime boolean results (#663)

## Motivation

Every boolean-producing operation (comparisons, equality checks,
`std.isXxx`) allocates a new `Val.True` or `Val.False` instance. Since
these are stateless value objects, we can use singleton instances to
eliminate millions of allocations in comparison-heavy workloads.

## Key Design Decision

Introduce `Val.staticTrue` and `Val.staticFalse` singleton constants
with a synthetic position, and a `Val.bool(v: Boolean)` factory method.
All comparison operators and stdlib boolean-returning functions use
these singletons instead of allocating new instances.

## Modification

- Added `staticTrue`/`staticFalse` singletons in `Val.scala`
- Added `Val.bool(v: Boolean)` factory method
- Updated `Evaluator.scala` comparison operators to use `Val.bool()`
- Updated stdlib functions (`TypeModule`, `ArrayModule`, `ObjectModule`,
`StringModule`) to use `Val.bool()`
- Updated `PrettyYamlRenderer.scala` to use
`Val.staticTrue`/`Val.staticFalse`

## Benchmark Results

### JMH Regression Suite (3 warmup, 3 measurement iterations)

| Benchmark | Master | This PR | Change |
|-----------|--------|---------|--------|
| comparison2 | 71.358 ± 13.411 ms/op | 58.837 ± 32.655 ms/op |
**-17.5%** |
| bench.02 | 47.044 ± 3.074 ms/op | 47.038 ± 1.115 ms/op | neutral ✅ |
| realistic2 | 66.034 ± 7.196 ms/op | 68.177 ± 8.385 ms/op | neutral ✅ |

### Scala Native Hyperfine (warmup 5, runs 15)

| Benchmark | sjsonnet master | This PR | jrsonnet | vs master | vs
jrsonnet |

|-----------|----------------|---------|----------|-----------|-------------|
| comparison2 | 173.3 ± 2.4 ms | 150.6 ± 2.2 ms | 206.1 ± 1.7 ms |
**1.15x faster** | **1.37x faster** ✅ |
| bench.02 | 73.3 ± 1.9 ms | 72.8 ± 2.8 ms | 116.6 ± 2.3 ms | neutral |
1.60x faster ✅ |
| realistic2 | 296.1 ± 1.9 ms | 296.2 ± 2.2 ms | 98.7 ± 2.4 ms | neutral
| — |

## Analysis

The improvement is most pronounced in comparison-heavy workloads.
`comparison2` creates millions of boolean values through array element
comparisons, making allocation elimination highly effective.

- **JVM**: 17.5% improvement on comparison2
- **Native**: 15% improvement on comparison2, sjsonnet **1.37x faster**
than jrsonnet
- No regressions on any other benchmark

## References

- Upstream jit branch exploration:
[he-pin/sjsonnet@jit](https://github.com/he-pin/sjsonnet/tree/jit)
- jrsonnet benchmark reference:
[benchmarks.adoc](https://github.com/CertainLach/jrsonnet/blob/master/docs/benchmarks.adoc)

## Result

15% native improvement on comparison2. sjsonnet is **1.37x faster** than
jrsonnet (Rust) on comparison-heavy workloads.

commit.message:
Add CodeQL config to exclude .vscode-test/ from analysis (#5456)

The .vscode-test/ directory is populated at CI test runtime by the
vscode-test npm package, which downloads a VS Code binary to run
integration tests. This directory is already in .gitignore and
contains third-party VS Code/extension code (not PowerShell source).

S360/CodeQL was flagging CodeQL.SM04514 'Weak hashes' in:
  .vscode-test/.../ms-vscode.js-debug/src/bootloader.js

That file belongs to the VS Code JavaScript Debugger extension
(ms-vscode.js-debug), owned by the VS Code team. Adding a CodeQL
paths-ignore config prevents the scanner from analyzing runtime
artifacts that are outside PowerShell's ownership and control.

Resolves: ADO #34872363

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-retry with resumeSession when agent evicts stale cached session

When the Copilot agent subprocess drops a session from its internal map
(due to inactivity/memory pressure), our cached CopilotSession object
becomes stale. Previously this caused permanent "Session not found"
errors until server restart.

Now _doWork() catches "Session not found" from session.send(), evicts
the stale cache entry, calls resumeSession() to re-register with the
agent, and retries the send — self-healing without restart.

commit.message:
fix: three Windows installer bugs — double-run, stale clone, theme never written

Bug 1: Switch double-run
  PowerShell switch is case-insensitive by default, so typing 'A' matched
  BOTH the "A" and "a" cases, running the entire workflow twice.
  Fix: switch -CaseSensitive + merged A/a into a single pattern case
  ($_ -in "A","a") so either capitalisation triggers one run.

Bug 2: Stale clone missing new files
  Symlink-Dotfiles only called git clone when the target directory was
  absent.  If the user had a clone from before themes/windows/ and
  windows/settings.json were added, those files were never downloaded.
  Fix: git pull --ff-only when the directory already exists (both the
  "dotfilesDir was null" and "dotfilesDir was known" code paths now pull).

Bug 3: Theme and WT colour scheme never written without repo files
  Configure-WindowsTerminal: fell back to font-only when settings.json
  was absent.  Now embeds the full Catppuccin Mocha colour scheme as a
  PSCustomObject so font + scheme are always applied to WT settings.json
  regardless of whether the repo clone is available.
  Configure-PowerShell: only deployed theme.ps1 when the repo catppuccin-
  mocha.ps1 existed.  Now writes the full theme inline (matching the repo
  file byte-for-byte) when the repo file is missing, so PSReadLine always
  gets Catppuccin Mocha colours on first run.

r600: add Terakan Shader ABI Contract (Phase 3 design)

Formal hardware resource allocation map for TeraScale-2 Vulkan driver,
validated by two independent rubber-duck reviews:

- KCACHE bank map: 0-13 UBOs, 14 robustness metadata, 15 push constants
- Write robustness via dedicated bank 14 buffer (not push constants)
  avoids ABI break, cache invalidation, and bank 15 indexing restriction
- Read robustness is zero-cost (hardware-enforced by VTX/TEX units)
- Saturating bounds check guards ALL MEM_RAT ops (stores + atomics)
- 4-phase pass discipline: resource-agnostic → layout → ABI → cleanup
- System value 3-tier folding: compile-time → layout-derived → runtime
- Complete resource/sampler/UAV slot budgets from header analysis

Findings from EVERGREEN_OOB_ROBUSTNESS_FINDINGS.md (Phase 5 probes)
drive the read/write asymmetry that makes this architecture viable.

Adding "winapp ui" for UI automation (#380)

## Description

Adds a new winapp ui command group that enables UI automation for
Windows apps using Microsoft UI Automation (UIA). This allows
developers and AI agents to programmatically discover, inspect, and
interact with UI elements across any Windows application.

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| ---------------- |
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| ui list-windows | List visible windows for an app |
| ui status | Connect to an app and print session info |
| ui inspect | Dump the UI tree (or ancestors/subtree) |
| ui search | Find elements by text query |
| ui invoke | Activate an element via UIA patterns (Invoke, Toggle,
Select, ExpandCollapse) |
| ui click | Click via mouse input at screen coordinates (fallback for
non-invocable controls) |
| ui focus | Move keyboard focus to an element |
| ui set-value | Set text/value on editable controls |
| ui get-property | Read UIA property values from an element |
| ui get-focused | Report the currently focused element |
| ui screenshot | Save a PNG of a window or element |
| ui scroll | Scroll a container (up/down/left/right/top/bottom) |
| ui scroll-into-view | Scroll a target element into view |
| ui wait-for | Wait for element appearance, disappearance, or property
change |

## Key design decisions

- Semantic slug selectors — inspect and search output stable, shell-safe
element slugs (e.g., btn-close-d1a2) that can be passed
 directly to other commands
- Flexible app targeting — Target apps by process name, window title,
PID, or HWND (--window for stability)
- Invokable ancestor fallback — search and invoke can surface/use a
parent element that supports invoke when the matched child
does not
- Mouse input fallback — click uses real mouse injection (SetCursorPos +
SendInput) for controls that don't expose UIA invoke
patterns
- Testable architecture — UIA and session services are behind interfaces
with fakes for unit testing

## Example usage

 winapp ui inspect --app notepad
 winapp ui search "Save" --app myapp
 winapp ui invoke btn-save-d1a2 --app myapp
 winapp ui screenshot --app myapp --output screen.png
 winapp ui wait-for btn-done-f3c1 --app myapp --timeout 10

commit.message:
feat: reject ambiguous scheduleAt timestamps without timezone (#37)

* Ⓜ fix(ci): skip MCP Registry publish when no new release

semantic-release may find no relevant changes and skip releasing.
The MCP Registry publish step ran unconditionally, causing a
'duplicate version' error when the version was already published.

Now the Release step compares package.json version before/after
semantic-release and sets a new-release output. The Install and
Publish steps only run when a new version was actually created.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ⓜ feat: reject ambiguous scheduleAt timestamps without timezone

Bare local datetimes like 2025-01-15T14:30:00 are silently interpreted
as UTC by the JS Date constructor, which is almost never what the caller
intended. Add a pre-parse check that requires an explicit timezone
designator (Z or ±HH:MM) and return a helpful error message pointing
the user to the fix.

Closes #33

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ⓜ fix: add type assertions to timezone offset tests

The 'result' from action.execute() is typed as 'unknown', requiring
explicit type assertions when accessing properties. The existing
scheduleMessage test had the assertion but the two new timezone
offset tests were missing it, causing TS18046 on strict builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add locks to buildtrees and packages. (#1979)

This is a followup on https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg-tool/pull/1938
Resolves
https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/pull/51004#issuecomment-4195577101

@autoantwort points out that our MSBuild integration leaves the default
buildtrees location and only overrides the installed root:


https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/blob/81e3c888ef717a026eb57bb2f3cb0dbd30246271/scripts/buildsystems/msbuild/vcpkg.targets#L183-L190

I initially tried a solution which would get `buildtrees()` out of
`VcpkgPaths` entirely and pass around something that had both the data
and effectively encapsulated that things had been locked, but changes to
make that happen were starting to get huge, and I saw that I had already
accidentally created deadlocks by being inconsistent about the order of
taking the lock on the installed tree vs. buildtrees and packages, so I
gave up on it. (
https://github.com/BillyONeal/vcpkg-tool/commit/0a4b9ef757f68fd784bc14994bb7fb85ecd182eb
)

commit.message:
Add 'winapp new' command for creating WinUI 3 projects and items (#411)

## Summary

Adds a new top-level \winapp new\ command that scaffolds WinUI 3
projects and items using the
\Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.WinUI.CSharp.Templates\ package.

## What it does

- **Interactive mode**: Presents a template picker and name prompt when
run without arguments
- **Non-interactive mode**: \winapp new winui -n MyApp\ creates a
project with defaults
- **Context-aware**: Outside a \.csproj\ directory shows project
templates (winui, winui-navview, etc.); inside one shows item templates
(winui-page, winui-window, etc.)
- **Auto-installs/updates** the template package on each invocation
- **Dynamic discovery**: Reads template.json from the nupkg — no
hardcoded template names or parameters
- **Post-creation setup**: Configures RuntimeIdentifier for current arch
and adds the WinApp build tools NuGet package
- **Next steps**: Shows \cd <name>\ + \dotnet run\ after project
creation
- **Unique default names**: Avoids collisions with existing directories
(\winui-app\, \winui-app2\, etc.)

## Architecture

Introduces an \ITemplateProvider\ abstraction for future C++ template
support:

\\\
ITemplateProvider (per-language contract)
  └── DotNetTemplateProvider  (C# via dotnet new)
  └── [future] CppTemplateProvider

ITemplateService (composite, aggregates all providers)
  └── NewCommand talks to this
\\\

Adding a new language later is a single DI registration line.

## Usage examples

\\\ ash
# Interactive
winapp new

# Create a blank WinUI app
winapp new winui

# Create a NavigationView app with a name
winapp new winui-navview -n MyApp

# Add a page to an existing project (from inside the project dir)
winapp new winui-page -n SettingsPage

# Pass extra dotnet new arguments
winapp new winui -n MyApp -- --dotnet-version net9.0
\\\

## Files

### New (7 files)
- \Commands/NewCommand.cs\ — Command with interactive + non-interactive
flows
- \Services/ITemplateProvider.cs\ — Per-language provider interface
- \Services/DotNetTemplateProvider.cs\ — C# provider (reads nupkg, wraps
dotnet new)
- \Services/ITemplateService.cs\ — Composite service interface
- \Services/TemplateService.cs\ — Aggregates all providers
- \Models/TemplateInfo.cs\ — Template/parameter models + JSON source-gen
- \WinApp.Cli.Tests/NewCommandTests.cs\ — 15 tests

### Modified
- \WinAppRootCommand.cs\, \HostBuilderExtensions.cs\ — DI registration
- \README.md\, \docs/usage.md\, \docs/guides/dotnet.md\, setup skill —
Documentation
- Autogenerated: \cli-schema.json\, \
pm-usage.md\, \winapp-commands.ts\, \SKILL.md\

## Tests

15 new tests covering:
- CLI schema registration
- JSON deserialization (including trailing commas from real Microsoft
templates)
- Nupkg template discovery (project/item types, parameter extraction,
invalid templates)
- Composite service (provider aggregation, create routing, unknown
template errors)
- Unique default name generation (directory/file collision handling)
- Non-interactive error paths

---------

commit.message:
fix: PS5 parse failure when temp script read as Windows-1252

Root cause: irm|iex elevation block downloaded the script via
Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile (raw bytes, no BOM).  PS5 then read
the temp file using the system code page (Windows-1252).  The
UTF-8 em dash (U+2014 = E2 80 94) contains byte 0x94 which maps
to a closing double-quote in Windows-1252 -- this terminated string
literals mid-line, cascading into dozens of parse errors.

Fixes applied:
1. Replace em dashes in string literals with ASCII hyphens.
   Four Write-Status/array strings on lines 442, 472, 594, 628, 634.
   (Em dashes in inline # comments are harmless and left alone.)

2. Temp-file downloads now write with UTF-8 BOM so PS5 correctly
   identifies and reads the file as UTF-8 rather than guessing the
   system code page.  Both download sites updated (elevation block
   and PS7-upgrade block):
     Before: Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile $TempScript
     After:  $response = Invoke-WebRequest ...; WriteAllText with
             [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($true)  (BOM = true)

3. Elevation block now prefers C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh.exe
   when launching the elevated process.  If PS7 is already installed
   the elevated process runs in PS7 (full UTF-8 support, no issue).
   Falls back to powershell.exe (PS5) only if PS7 is absent.

commit.message:
fix: add Request-Id and Request-Context to CORS allowed headers, remove double-CORS in production (#227)

The Application Insights browser SDK (enableCorsCorrelation: true) sends
Request-Id and Request-Context headers on cross-origin requests. These
were missing from both the FastAPI CORSMiddleware and the Azure Container
App CORS policy, causing preflight failures.

Also removes CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS from the backend env vars in production
deployments to prevent double-CORS headers (Container App CORS handles
preflights at the proxy level; FastAPI CORSMiddleware adding a second set
of headers on actual requests risks duplicate Access-Control-Allow-Origin
values which browsers reject).
